events.title: Indy Winter Farmers Market
events.description: Come out to the Indy Winter Farmers Market every Saturday from 9am – 12:30pm, until April 25th. With over 50 vendors, there is a little something for everyone!

events.title: March Madness Watch Party
events.description:
Get ready for the best time of the year at The AMP! 🏀 
Join us March 17 through April 6 as we stream all the tournament action on the big screens. Grab your crew, order from your favorite AMP vendors, and catch every buzzer-beater, upset, and bracket-buster with us. 
We’ll have food and drink specials, games, and entertainment throughout the tournament — plus live DJ sets on March 20, March 27, April 3, and April 6 to keep the energy high between matchups. 
Basketball. Food. Fun.

events.title: H7 Network, Carmel, IN
events.description:
H7 Indianapolis IN Business Networking
**Wolfies Grill - Carmel**
*1162 Keystone Way S, Carmel, IN 46032, USA*

H7 Carmel is our weekly networking meeting open to local B2B and B2C professionals. Experience the distinct, structural, difference from other networking groups, associations, and chambers. Our community of meetings are led by Champion Leadership.

***All meetings welcome visitors, we'd love to show you what we're all about!***

\*\*\*Meeting Contact: \*\*\*

**Seth Wilson** *at* ***31

events.title: Upcoming events.
events.description: Why do some investors consistently make better calls—especially when decisions are fast, uncertain, and high-stakes? Research suggests the answer isn’t only experience or pattern recognition, but when those decisions are made. This workshop draws on Regan Stevenson’s research to examine how subtle and other unexpected drivers, such as investors’ biological timing preferences, influence risk tolerance, judgment quality, and investment outcomes.

events.title: Does Timing Create Better Investors? What Biological Timing Reveals About Investment Decisions
events.description: Why do some investors consistently make better calls—especially when decisions are fast, uncertain, and high-stakes? Research suggests the answer isn’t only experience or pattern recognition, but when those decisions are made. This workshop draws on Regan Stevenson’s research to examine how subtle and other unexpected drivers, such as investors’ biological timing preferences, influence risk tolerance, judgment quality, and investment outcomes.

events.title: Indy.rb - The Indianapolis Ruby Brigade Monthly Meetup
events.description:
Indyrb - Indianapolis Ruby Brigade
Join us to talk about Ruby, work projects, hack on something, or just get a drink or bite & enjoy the company.

We are mixing it up again and meeting this month at The Ale Emporium Castleton location
8617 Allisonville Rd
Indianapolis, 46250
https://www.ale-emporium.com/Locations/indianapolis

events.title: April 2026 CINLUG Meeting — Using AI Wisely In Technical Workflows
events.description:
CINLUG | Central Indiana Linux Users Group
The tech industry is racing toward an artificial intelligence-driven (AI-driven) future, but organizations face a critical choice in how they deploy it. Many are making a fatal miscalculation: trying to replace self-directed domain experts with a "fleet of interns". They are deploying AI as an autonomous executor, leaving management in the impossible position of directing a swarm of eager but context-blind digital toddlers.

In this talk, Red Hat Techni
